title: Ammunition pressure
principles: Ammunition pressure describes how quickly an attack mode can deplete its available reserve under a defined consumption pattern.
Reserve cost and consumption-event frequency must remain separate because neither value alone establishes ammunition pressure.
The ammunition-consuming event must be explicitly identified and must not be inferred from fire rate, damage ticks, pellets, or delivery type.
Reserve capacity delays depletion but does not establish efficient or sustainable ammunition use.
Ammo pickup describes recovery per pickup event and does not establish pickup availability or long-duration sustainability.
Maximum reserve duration represents a standardized maximum-consumption scenario and must remain distinct from practical player use.
Ammunition pressure must be evaluated separately for attack modes with different costs or consumption patterns.

low: The supplied reserve, cost, and consumption frequency indicate limited pressure under the defined consumption scenario.
This classification does not guarantee indefinite operation without recovery.
moderate: Repeated use creates noticeable reserve pressure without making depletion clearly dominant under the supplied scenario.
elevated: The supplied consumption pattern can deplete the available reserve quickly relative to its capacity.
Pickup recovery may reduce this pressure but does not establish sustained availability.
regenerative: The ammunition resource recovers through an explicitly supplied regeneration model rather than ordinary pickup-dependent reserve replacement.
Regeneration delay, rate, and activation conditions must remain separate.
unlimited: The evaluated mode does not consume a finite ammunition reserve under the supplied mechanic.
Do not apply conventional reserve-pressure logic.
undetermined: The ammunition cost, consuming event, event frequency, or reserve model is not sufficiently defined.
Reserve capacity, pickup amount, or displayed fire rate alone is insufficient.

per_damage_tick_source_data: Normalize ammo_cost_per_damage_tick into ammunition cost per defined consumption event only when the source explicitly links consumption to damage ticks.
Do not infer that relationship from beam or continuous delivery.
player_limited_consumption: Treat the supplied maximum event frequency as an upper consumption bound rather than guaranteed practical consumption.
variable_consumption: Preserve separate costs or phases when ammunition consumption changes during charge, burst, held, Duplex, staged, or alternate-mode behavior.
Do not reduce variable consumption to one constant rate without explicit normalization.
pickup_recovery_available: Compare ammo pickup with documented consumption only as recovery per pickup event.
Do not infer how frequently pickups occur or whether recovery keeps pace during actual missions.
multi_mode_weapon: Evaluate ammunition pressure independently for every mode with a distinct cost, reserve interaction, or consumption frequency.

exceptions: Do not infer ammunition pressure from fire rate alone.
Do not assume that multishot consumes additional ammunition.
Do not infer ammunition cost from pellets, fire iterations, damage components, beam classification, or radial behavior.
Do not include magazine reload interruption in ammunition-pressure classification.
Do not treat ammo pickup amount as proof of long-duration ammunition sustainability.
